mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 5.8% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and increases in CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating mixed rate expectations that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined signal drops below 0.05 in any 3-year window or directional consistency fails in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units signal strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns during expansionary regimes as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline by 2% in 60-day windows when HOUST exceeds 1.2 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k indicate robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns as corporate hiring supports consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.5 signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases when INDPRO declines by 1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) above $445 billion indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns as corporate revenues improve.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline by 1.5% in 20-day windows when RSAFS exceeds $445 billion.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining CPI (CPIAUCSL) signals healthy economic expansion with disinflation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined signal drops below 0.04 in any 3-year window or directional consistency fails in 35%+ of cases.
